name: Gateron KS-20 Magnetic Hall-Effect (Low-Profile)
summary: The ONLY low-profile hall-effect switch available — analog sensing in a compact form factor, but limited availability
pinDescription: VCC, GND, Analog Signal. Proprietary pin layout — exact dimensions not publicly documented.
hotswapNote: Uses Gateron's low-profile magnetic hot-swap sockets — NOT compatible with Kailh Choc sockets
scanningNote: Same ADC + multiplexer architecture as KS-33. See KS-33 design notes for scanning details.
footprintNote: No official KiCad footprint. Pin positions must be measured from physical samples. Very limited community documentation.
designNotes: The ONLY low-profile hall-effect switch that exists in any purchasable form.
Very limited individual availability — primarily supplied to OEM keyboard manufacturers.
Sometimes appears on AliExpress or Taobao but supply is inconsistent.
Same ADC + multiplexer scanning architecture as the full-size KS-33.
No official datasheet with pin dimensions — requires physical measurement.
Production status uncertain — Gateron's product page has been inconsistent about this model.
If you need guaranteed low-profile + analog, consider using Kailh Choc v1 (mechanical) and adding separate hall-effect sensors to the PCB.
features: Low-profile form factor (~12mm total height vs 18.5mm for MX)
True analog output — same rapid trigger capability as full-size hall-effect
Contactless operation — no mechanical contacts to wear out
Reduced travel distance (3.0mm) for faster key presses
concerns: VERY limited availability — may be difficult or impossible to source
No public datasheet or KiCad footprint — significant reverse-engineering needed
OEM-focused product — not designed for individual/DIY purchase
Exact dimensions may vary between production batches
Requires same complex ADC scanning as KS-33 (no standard matrix)
ZMK does not natively support analog scanning
Custom low-profile hot-swap sockets may be even harder to source than the switches
